attractions.name: Seward Highway
attractions.aka: AK-1/AK-9; Turnagain Arm National Scenic Byway
attractions.season: Open year-round; occasional avalanche closures Nov-Apr
attractions.blurb: 127mi Anchorage-Seward along Turnagain Arm then over Turnagain Pass; the only triple-designated byway in the US.
attractions.tip: Budget 4h, not the 2.5h Google says - the pullouts are the point. Coord is Turnagain Pass rest area, MP 68.

attractions.name: George Parks Highway
attractions.season: Open and plowed year-round
attractions.blurb: 323mi Anchorage-Fairbanks spine past Denali State Park and the national park entrance; paved the whole way.
attractions.tip: Coord is Hurricane Gulch Bridge (MP 174), 260ft above the creek - pullouts on both ends, no shoulder on the span.

attractions.name: Glenn Highway
attractions.aka: AK-1; Glenn Highway National Scenic Byway
attractions.season: Year-round; winter ground blizzards near Sheep Mtn
attractions.blurb: 189mi Anchorage-Glennallen past Matanuska Glacier, Lion Head and the Chugach/Talkeetna wall of peaks.
attractions.tip: Coord is Eureka Summit (MP 129, 3322ft), the highest point and best 360 pullout. Fuel at Glennallen or Palmer only.

attractions.name: Richardson Highway
attractions.season: Year-round; Thompson Pass closes in storms
attractions.blurb: 368mi Valdez-Fairbanks over Thompson and Isabel Passes; follows the 1898 gold trail and the pipeline.
attractions.tip: Coord is Keystone Canyon (MP 13.5) - Bridal Veil and Horsetail Falls plus the hand-cut 1900s Goat Trail tunnel.

attractions.name: Denali Highway
attractions.season: Opened May 1 in 2026; maintained to Oct 1, then closed all winter
attractions.blurb: 135mi mostly-gravel road across the south flank of the Alaska Range; caribou, eskers, near-zero traffic.
attractions.tip: Coord is Maclaren Summit (MP 37, 4086ft), Alaska's 2nd-highest road pass. Most rental car contracts ban this road.
attractions.duration_note: 6-8h end to end at 35mph; no services mid-route

attractions.name: Dalton Highway
attractions.season: Open year-round but no winter services; summer dust/mud
attractions.cost: Free (fuel only at Yukon Crossing, Coldfoot, Deadhorse)
attractions.blurb: 414mi gravel truck road Livengood to Deadhorse crossing the Arctic Circle, Brooks Range and North Slope.
attractions.tip: Coord is Atigun Pass (MP 244, 4739ft), the highest road pass in AK. Carry 2 spares - most rentals forbid this road.
attractions.duration_note: 2 days each way minimum from Fairbanks

attractions.name: Sterling Highway
attractions.season: Year-round; heavy July combat-fishing traffic
attractions.blurb: 143mi Tern Lake to Homer past Kenai River canyon, Cook Inlet volcanoes and the Kachemak Bay drop-in.
attractions.tip: Coord is Baycrest Overlook (MP 168.5) - the first full view of Homer Spit and the glaciers behind Kachemak Bay.
attractions.duration_note: 4-5h Tern Lake to Homer with stops

attractions.name: Steese Highway
attractions.season: Year-round to Central; Eagle Summit drifts shut in winter storms
attractions.blurb: 162mi Fairbanks to Circle on the Yukon, half gravel, over two alpine summits and old Circle District dredge country.
attractions.tip: Coord is Eagle Summit (MP 107, 3624ft) - one of the few road spots south of the Circle with true midnight sun on Jun 21.
attractions.duration_note: full day round trip from Fairbanks

attractions.name: Elliott Highway
attractions.aka: AK-2; Fox to Manley Hot Springs
attractions.season: Year-round to Livengood; gravel and rough beyond
attractions.blurb: 152mi ridge-running road from Fox to Manley Hot Springs; also the only approach to the Dalton, which splits off at MP 73.
attractions.tip: Coord is Wickersham Dome trailhead (MP 28) - easiest alpine tundra walk near Fairbanks and the White Mtns viewpoint.

attractions.name: Taylor Highway
attractions.season: Open roughly mid-Apr to mid-Oct; gated and unmaintained in winter
attractions.blurb: 160mi narrow ridge road from the Alaska Hwy through Chicken to Eagle on the Yukon; gravel past MP 33.
attractions.tip: Coord is the Mount Fairplay wayside (MP 35), the big Fortymile country overlook. Fuel at Chicken is cash-heavy and dear.

attractions.name: Top of the World Highway
attractions.aka: Yukon Hwy 9 / Poker Creek border crossing
attractions.season: Border station open May 22 - Sep 15, 2026, 8am-6pm Alaska time daily
attractions.cost: Free (Yukon River ferry at Dawson is free)
attractions.blurb: 79mi ridgeline gravel road from Jack Wade Junction to Dawson City, running along the crest above treeline the whole way.
attractions.tip: Poker Creek is the northernmost US border post and shuts at 6pm AK time sharp - miss it and you are sleeping on the ridge.
attractions.duration_note: 3-4h Chicken to Dawson City incl. border and river ferry

attractions.name: Nabesna Road
attractions.season: Open summer; several creeks ford the road and rise after rain
attractions.blurb: 42mi dead-end gravel road into the quiet north side of Wrangell-St. Elias; volcanoes, trailheads, almost no people.
attractions.tip: Stop at Slana Ranger Station first for creek-crossing conditions; most cars are fine to MP 29, high clearance beyond.

attractions.name: McCarthy Road
attractions.aka: Chitina to McCarthy; old CR&NW railbed
attractions.season: Summer road; gated/unplowed most winters beyond Chitina
attractions.cost: Free; $5-10/day parking at the footbridge lot
attractions.blurb: 59mi gravel road on an abandoned railbed to the McCarthy footbridge, gateway to Kennecott Mines.
attractions.tip: Coord is Kuskulana Bridge (MP 17), a 1910 trestle 238ft above the gorge. 2.5-3h each way; no vehicles cross to McCarthy.
attractions.duration_note: 3h each way from Chitina; do not day-trip it from Anchorage

attractions.name: Hatcher Pass Road
attractions.season: Pass itself typically open ~Jul 1 to mid-Sep; Palmer side plowed to Independence Mine year-round
attractions.cost: Free; $5 state park day-use at Independence Mine
attractions.blurb: Alpine road over a 3886ft pass in the Talkeetnas linking Palmer to Willow; gravel and one-lane on the Willow side.
attractions.tip: The through-route opens late - call before planning a loop. Palmer-side pavement to Independence Mine works any summer day.
attractions.duration_note: half-day loop from Palmer or Wasilla

attractions.name: Skilak Lake Road
attractions.season: Open year-round, unmaintained gravel; best Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: 19mi gravel loop off the Sterling Hwy through Kenai NWR; campgrounds, moose, and a string of short lake trails.
attractions.tip: Skilak Lookout Trail (2.2mi RT, 750ft) is the payoff stop; both loop junctions are Sterling Hwy MP 58 and MP 75.

attractions.name: Denali Park Road
attractions.aka: the Park Road; Savage River to East Fork
attractions.season: Private vehicles to Mile 15 late May-mid Sep; CLOSED at Mile 43 all of 2026 for the Pretty Rocks bridge project
attractions.blurb: 92mi road into Denali NP. In 2026 you drive to Mile 15 (Savage River); buses run only to Mile 43 (East Fork).
attractions.tip: Polychrome, Eielson, Wonder Lake and Kantishna are unreachable by road in 2026; full service is targeted for 2027.
attractions.duration_note: 4-6h for the Mile 43 bus round trip

attractions.name: Copper River Highway
attractions.aka: Cordova road to the Million Dollar Bridge
attractions.season: Summer only; road ends at Mile 36 since the 2011 Bridge 339 washout
attractions.cost: Free (you must ferry or fly your car to Cordova)
attractions.blurb: Gravel road across the Copper River delta to the 1910 Million Dollar Bridge between Childs and Sheridan Glaciers.
attractions.tip: The bridge is the turnaround - the road beyond is gone. Best shorebird migration is the first week of May at Hartney Bay.

attractions.name: Portage Glacier Highway & Whittier Tunnel
attractions.season: Year-round; summer 2026 last entry to Whittier 10:30pm, last exit 11:00pm
attractions.cost: $13 toll per 2-axle car, eastbound only; return is free
attractions.blurb: 2.5mi single-lane tunnel under Maynard Mtn, the longest highway tunnel in North America and shared with the railroad.
attractions.tip: Traffic alternates: into Whittier on the :30, out on the :00. Arrive 15 min early and check the schedule - you can get stuck.
attractions.duration_note: 30-40 min from Girdwood incl. staging wait

attractions.name: Beluga Point
attractions.season: Year-round
attractions.blurb: Rock-outcrop pullout 17mi south of Anchorage; the classic Turnagain Arm stop for belugas, bore tides and Dall sheep.
attractions.tip: Belugas chase eulachon here late May and silvers in Aug. The bore arrives ~2h after Anchorage low tide - check the tables.

attractions.name: Bird Point Scenic Overlook
attractions.season: Year-round
attractions.blurb: Big paved wayside with elevated viewing decks over the mudflats; the best public spot to watch a Turnagain Arm bore tide.
attractions.tip: The bore reaches Bird Point about 2h15m after low tide at Anchorage; biggest 2-3 days after a new or full moon.
attractions.duration_note: 30 min; longer if timing a bore tide

attractions.name: Windy Corner
attractions.season: Year-round; sheep lowest May-Jun
attractions.blurb: Cliff band where Dall sheep come to a mineral lick right beside the road - roughly the only place they are seen at sea level.
attractions.tip: Park in the gravel pullout on the water side and walk back; stopping in the travel lane here causes wrecks and tickets.

attractions.name: Sheep Mountain Overlook
attractions.season: Year-round; lodge and cafe open most of the year
attractions.blurb: Banded red-and-cream mountain above the Glenn Highway; a reliable Dall sheep glassing spot with a lodge and cafe at the pullout.
attractions.tip: Bring binoculars - the sheep are white dots on the upper slopes, not roadside. Good coffee/pie stop breaking Anchorage-Valdez.

attractions.name: Thompson Pass
attractions.season: Year-round but storm-closed in winter; snowiest place in Alaska
attractions.blurb: 2678ft alpine gap 26mi above Valdez holding US snowfall records (~975in in 1952-53); wildflower tundra in July.
attractions.tip: Worthington Glacier is 2mi north at MP 28.7 with a short paved path. Fog eats the view - check the Valdez webcam first.

attractions.name: Denali View South
attractions.aka: Denali Viewpoint South, Parks Highway MP 135
attractions.season: Road open year-round; wayside services summer only
attractions.blurb: State park wayside with mounted spotting scopes and interpretive panels 40mi from Denali - the best roadside view of the peak.
attractions.tip: Only about a third of summer days show the mountain; early morning is clearest. Free, no reservation, big-rig parking.

attractions.name: Denali View North
attractions.aka: Denali Viewpoint North Campground, Parks Highway MP 162.7
attractions.season: Campground open roughly late May - mid Sep
attractions.blurb: Quiet north-end Denali State Park wayside with a wide Alaska Range panorama and 20 first-come campsites.
attractions.tip: Far emptier than Denali View South and it holds evening alpenglow longer; last reliable Denali view before Broad Pass.

attractions.name: Broad Pass
attractions.season: Year-round
attractions.blurb: Broad tundra saddle at 2300ft dividing Cook Inlet from Yukon drainages; also the Alaska Railroad's high point.
attractions.tip: Peak tundra color mid-Aug to early Sep. Summit Lake pullouts here are the best photo spot; wide open, no trees blocking.

attractions.name: Rainbow Ridge
attractions.season: Year-round; colors best in low sun
attractions.blurb: Banded red, orange and green Alaska Range slopes right above the Richardson Highway north of Isabel Pass.
attractions.tip: Colors pop in early morning or after rain; several gravel pullouts. Combine with the Gulkana Glacier view at Isabel Pass.

attractions.name: Potter Marsh Boardwalk
attractions.aka: Anchorage Coastal Wildlife Refuge, Seward Hwy MP 117
attractions.season: Boardwalk open year-round; birds May-Sep, salmon Jul-Aug
attractions.blurb: 1550ft boardwalk over a marsh at the very start of the Seward Highway; trumpeter swans, arctic terns, moose, spawning salmon.
attractions.tip: First stop leaving Anchorage and the easiest wildlife on the whole drive. Best light and bird activity before 9am.

attractions.name: Tangle Lakes
attractions.season: Accessible only when the Denali Highway is open, roughly mid-May to Oct 1
attractions.blurb: Chain of tundra lakes on the Denali Highway inside a 226,000-acre archaeological district with 10,000-year-old sites.
attractions.tip: Last pavement heading west is just past here - two BLM campgrounds, a canoe put-in and the Delta River portage start.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2h with a paddle or a walk

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Denali Star
attractions.season: Daily May 13 - Sep 17, 2026 (northbound to Sep 16)
attractions.blurb: 356mi flagship daily train; 12h Anchorage to Fairbanks via Wasilla, Talkeetna and Denali, with dome cars and dining.
attractions.tip: Book GoldStar upper-deck for the open-air platform. Anchorage-Talkeetna or Denali-Fairbanks are good half-day segments.

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Coastal Classic
attractions.season: Daily May 15 - Sep 13, 2026
attractions.blurb: 114mi, 4.5h run down Turnagain Arm then through the roadless Placer valley, Grandview and Kenai Mountains to Seward.
attractions.tip: The Grandview/Loop District stretch past Bartlett and Trail Glaciers has no road access - sit on the left going south.
attractions.duration_note: 6:45am south, 6pm return; pairs with a Kenai Fjords day cruise

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Glacier Discovery
attractions.season: Daily May 11 - Sep 14, 2026
attractions.blurb: Anchorage-Girdwood-Portage-Whittier plus the whistle-stop leg to Spencer Glacier and Grandview, dropping hikers and rafters.
attractions.tip: Spencer Glacier is only reachable by this train - 1.3mi walk to the lake, or book a float trip that ends at Portage.

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Hurricane Turn
attractions.season: Thu-Sun mid-May to mid-Sep; winter runs the 1st Thursday monthly Oct-May
attractions.blurb: One of America's last true flagstop trains: wave a white cloth anywhere between Talkeetna and Hurricane and it stops for you.
attractions.tip: Board at Talkeetna, not Anchorage. No dining car - bring food. It crosses the 296ft Hurricane Gulch railroad bridge.

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Aurora Winter Train
attractions.season: Weekends mid-Sep to mid-May, plus select midweek runs in Feb-Mar
attractions.blurb: The winter version of the Denali Star: weekend-only Anchorage-Fairbanks service through snowbound Broad Pass.
attractions.tip: Sells out around aurora season and the Feb-Mar sled-dog races. Northbound Sat, southbound Sun; flagstops still honored.
attractions.duration_note: ~12h; pairs with a Fairbanks aurora stay

attractions.name: Anchorage Historic Rail Depot
attractions.season: Ticket office year-round; trains daily in summer
attractions.blurb: 1942 depot at 411 W 1st Ave, the railroad's headquarters and the boarding point for every summer route.
attractions.tip: Locomotive No. 1, the 1907 engine that built the line, sits out front. Bag drop opens 60-90 min before departure.

attractions.name: Whittier Terminal
attractions.aka: AMHS ferry dock, Alaska Railroad depot and cruise pier
attractions.season: Ferry and rail service May-Sep; tunnel access year-round
attractions.cost: Free to visit; ferry and cruise fares vary
attractions.blurb: One small harbor combining the Prince William Sound ferry berth, the railroad dock and the cruise pier - the AK transfer hub.
attractions.tip: Everything here is gated by the tunnel schedule. Ferry check-in closes 90 min before sailing with a vehicle.
attractions.duration_note: allow 2h buffer for tunnel plus check-in

attractions.name: AMHS Southeast Mainline
attractions.aka: Bellingham/Ketchikan - Wrangell - Petersburg - Juneau - Haines - Skagway
attractions.season: Summer schedule May 1 - Sep 30, 2026; year-round at reduced frequency
attractions.cost: ~$400-500 walk-on Bellingham-Haines; vehicles and cabins much more
attractions.blurb: Inside Passage backbone: M/V Columbia and Kennicott link Bellingham to Ketchikan, Wrangell, Petersburg, Juneau and Skagway.
attractions.tip: Bellingham-Haines is ~3 days. No Prince Rupert BC service in 2026. Deck camping is allowed - bring a tent and duct tape.
attractions.duration_note: 38h Bellingham-Ketchikan; 2-3 days to the north end

attractions.name: AMHS Cross-Gulf Route
attractions.season: Only a handful of sailings, roughly monthly, May-Sep
attractions.blurb: The only way to drive your car between Southeast Alaska and the mainland road system without going through Canada.
attractions.tip: Sails only a few times a season and sells out early - it is the linchpin of any Southeast-to-Southcentral driving loop.
attractions.duration_note: ~2.5 days Juneau to Whittier via Yakutat

attractions.name: AMHS Prince William Sound Route
attractions.season: Summer sailings May-Sep; reduced in winter
attractions.cost: ~$120 walk-on Whittier-Valdez; ~$160 more for a car
attractions.blurb: Working ferry across Prince William Sound past Columbia Glacier; the only vehicle link to roadless Cordova.
attractions.tip: Whittier-Valdez lets you drive out on the Richardson instead of backtracking - the best loop shortcut in Southcentral.

attractions.name: AMHS Southwest / Aleutian Chain Route
attractions.aka: Homer - Kodiak - Chignik - Sand Point - King Cove - Cold Bay - False Pass - Akutan - Dutch Harbor
attractions.season: Chain sailings roughly monthly, late Apr - early Oct only
attractions.cost: ~$450-600 walk-on Homer to Dutch Harbor round trip
attractions.blurb: The legendary 'Trusty Tusty' run 1,000+mi down the Alaska Peninsula to Unalaska; volcanoes, seabirds, tiny fishing ports.
attractions.tip: Only a few round trips a season and vessel assignments shift year to year - confirm the ship and dates before booking flights.

attractions.name: AMHS Northern Panhandle Route
attractions.aka: M/V LeConte: Juneau - Hoonah - Angoon - Tenakee - Pelican - Kake
attractions.season: Year-round; more frequent May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Small-ferry milk run to the Tlingit and fishing villages of Chichagof and Admiralty Islands that no cruise ship visits.
attractions.tip: Hoonah (4.5h RT from Auke Bay) is the easiest day-trip; Tenakee and Pelican need an overnight because sailings are sparse.
attractions.duration_note: 4-5h to Hoonah; day trip or overnight

attractions.name: Auke Bay Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Year-round
attractions.blurb: Juneau's ferry terminal 14mi north of downtown, the hinge of the whole Southeast network and the stop for Glacier Bay connections.
attractions.tip: It is NOT downtown - allow 30 min drive and note there is no reliable transit at odd sailing hours. Vehicle check-in 90 min prior.

attractions.name: Homer Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Sailings roughly weekly to Kodiak in summer; monthly to the Chain
attractions.blurb: AMHS berth near the end of Homer Spit - the mainland road system's only ferry link to Kodiak and the Aleutian chain.
attractions.tip: Homer-Kodiak is ~10h overnight and far cheaper than flying with gear; sailings are sparse, so build slack into the plan.
attractions.duration_note: arrive 90 min early with a vehicle

attractions.name: Kodiak Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Year-round with reduced winter frequency
attractions.blurb: Downtown Kodiak ferry berth linking the island to Homer and, a few times a season, to the Aleutian chain run.
attractions.tip: Bringing your own vehicle over from Homer is the cheapest way to drive Kodiak's 100mi road system and hit remote beaches.
attractions.duration_note: arrive 90 min early with a vehicle

attractions.name: Haines Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Year-round; most sailings May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Terminal 5mi north of Haines; the Haines Highway runs from here over Chilkat Pass to the Alaska Hwy at Haines Junction.
attractions.tip: Ferry to Haines then drive the Haines Hwy over Chilkat Pass to reconnect with the road system - avoids the cross-gulf sailing.
attractions.duration_note: arrive 90 min early with a vehicle

attractions.name: Inter-Island Ferry Authority
attractions.aka: Ketchikan - Hollis, Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Daily year-round, one round trip a day
attractions.cost: ~$50 walk-on, ~$85 with a car, one way
attractions.blurb: Community-run 3h ferry from Ketchikan to Hollis, unlocking Prince of Wales Island's 1,500mi of road, karst caves and black bears.
attractions.tip: Separate from AMHS with its own website and reservation system - it is the only reliable POW connection and is often full.
attractions.duration_note: 3h each way; day trip is tight, plan an overnight
